Title: Amide conjugates of the jasmonate precursor cis-(+)-12-oxo-phytodienoic acid regulate its homeostasis during plant stress responses

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: induced upon wounding (reported from previous studies and discussed in context)
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Hydrolyzes multiple OPDA-amino acid conjugates to release cis-OPDA, thereby restricting OPDA-aa accumulation and contributing to cis-OPDA homeostasis during wounding.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Recombinant ILR1 hydrolyzed all six tested OPDA-aa, with preference for OPDA-Ala, OPDA-Asp, and OPDA-Glu. 2. OPDA-Ala and OPDA-Phe accumulated in ilr1-containing mutant backgrounds after wounding. 3. ILR1 redundantly contributes to rapid OPDA-aa turnover and active cis-OPDA regulation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant hydrolysis assay; wounding time-course metabolite profiling in single/triple knockout mutants
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altering ILR1 may change local cis-OPDA release dynamics during stress.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts redundantly with other ILR1/ILL hydrolases; classified as Common_Gene because it is a hydrolase controlling conjugate turnover rather than biosynthetic pathway synthesis of nutrient end product.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for tuning stress-responsive cis-OPDA release from inactive storage conjugates.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: induced upon wounding (reported from previous studies and discussed in context)
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Hydrolyzes several OPDA-amino acid conjugates to release cis-OPDA and limits OPDA-aa accumulation after wounding.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Recombinant IAR3 hydrolyzed mainly OPDA-Ala, OPDA-Asp, and OPDA-Val, and less efficiently OPDA-Glu and OPDA-Phe. 2. OPDA-Glu accumulated in iar3-2 mutant after wounding, supporting in planta function. 3. IAR3 redundantly contributes to OPDA-aa turnover and cis-OPDA homeostasis.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant hydrolysis assay; wounding time-course metabolite profiling in single/triple knockout mutants
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May alter localized stress signaling if edited.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In vitro and in vivo substrate preferences show partial redundancy with other family members.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate to modulate OPDA conjugate turnover under stress.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Hydrolyzes all six tested OPDA-aa and contributes to wound-induced OPDA-aa turnover to regenerate cis-OPDA.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Recombinant ILL2 hydrolyzed all six tested OPDA-aa well, especially OPDA-Ala, OPDA-Asp, and OPDA-Phe. 2. OPDA-Ala, OPDA-Phe, and OPDA-Glu accumulated in ill2-related mutant backgrounds after wounding. 3. ILL2 redundantly contributes to active cis-OPDA homeostasis.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant hydrolysis assay; wounding time-course metabolite profiling in single/triple knockout mutants
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could shift stress-growth balance by changing cis-OPDA release kinetics.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed together with JA-inducible amidohydrolases, but direct induction of ILL2 was not explicitly stated here.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to stabilize OPDA conjugate storage pools.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: induced upon wounding (reported from previous studies and discussed in context)
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Hydrolyzes OPDA-aa, especially OPDA-Glu and OPDA-Phe, suppresses accumulation of multiple OPDA-aa after wounding, and is required for proper spatial channeling of cis-OPDA at damaged leaf sites.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Recombinant ILL6 hydrolyzed OPDA-Glu most strongly and OPDA-Phe secondarily. 2. ill6-2 accumulated elevated OPDA-Ala, OPDA-Glu, OPDA-Ile, OPDA-Phe, and OPDA-Val after wounding. 3. DESI-MSI showed altered cis-OPDA spatial distribution in ill6-2, indicating ILL6 helps channel cis-OPDA at the wound site.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant hydrolysis assay; single knockout time-course metabolite profiling; DESI-MSI spatial imaging
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulating ILL6 could broadly alter local wound signaling and cis-OPDA distribution.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In vitro and in vivo effects were partly discrepant for some substrates, but in planta evidence strongly supports a major role in OPDA-aa turnover.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering local cis-OPDA homeostasis and wound-response dynamics.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: cis-OPDA amino acid conjugation / jasmonate homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: temporary storage/catabolism of cis-OPDA during stress responses
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of OPDA-Asp and contributes to stress-induced OPDA-amino acid homeostasis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant enzyme assay; gh3 sextuple knockout mutant feeding and wounding metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Recombinant GH3.1 conjugated cis-(±)-OPDA, with highest activity toward Asp. 2. OPDA-Asp formation was abolished in the gh3.1,2,3,4,5,6 sextuple mutant in planta. 3. Group II GH3 enzymes are uniquely responsible for OPDA-Asp formation and contribute to wound-induced OPDA-aa accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential lever to tune cis-OPDA homeostasis and stress signaling by editing OPDA conjugation capacity.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GH3 acyl acid amido synthetase, Group II
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for OPDA-Asp formation; not established as global rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: GH3 family acyl acid amido synthetase
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May alter jasmonate/cis-OPDA balance and stress-growth tradeoffs.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Conjugates cis-OPDA to Asp to form an amide-linked OPDA-Asp.

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Recombinant GH3.3 conjugated cis-(±)-OPDA, with highest activity toward Asp. 2. Group II GH3 enzymes are uniquely responsible for OPDA-Asp formation in planta. 3. GH3.3 localizes to both cytosol and nucleus, supporting these compartments as sites of OPDA conjugation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering compartmental control of cis-OPDA storage via amino acid conjugation.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: temporary storage/catabolism of cis-OPDA during stress responses

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: recombinant enzyme assay; mutant metabolite profiling inference
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Recombinant GH3.17 preferentially conjugated cis-(±)-OPDA with Glu. 2. Formation of non-Asp OPDA conjugates likely involves GH3.17 and Group III GH3s. 3. GH3-mediated OPDA conjugation contributes to cis-OPDA homeostasis under stress.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GH3 acyl acid amido synthetase, Group II
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: GH3 family acyl acid amido synthetase
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Changing OPDA buffering may affect stress signaling dynamics.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Conjugates cis-OPDA to Glu to form OPDA-Glu.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: cis-OPDA amino acid conjugation / jasmonate homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: temporary storage/catabolism of cis-OPDA during stress responses
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Conjugates cis-OPDA primarily with Glu and secondarily with Val, Ile, Phe, and Trp, contributing to non-Asp OPDA-aa formation.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtGH3.12 catalyzed conjugation of cis-(±)-OPDA mainly to Glu. 2. It also formed OPDA-Val, OPDA-Ile, OPDA-Phe, and OPDA-Trp at lower levels. 3. Group III GH3s participate in formation of non-Asp OPDA conjugates.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GH3 acyl acid amido synthetase, Group III
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: GH3 family acyl acid amido synthetase
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May alter both OPDA and JA conjugate pools because Group III GH3s are also active toward JA.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Conjugates cis-OPDA mainly with Glu and less efficiently with Val, Ile, Phe, and Trp.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: cis-OPDA amino acid conjugation / jasmonate homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: temporary storage/catabolism of cis-OPDA during stress responses
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Conjugates cis-OPDA primarily to Ala, Trp, and Ile, and secondarily to Phe, contributing to non-Asp OPDA-aa formation.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtGH3.14 formed OPDA-Ala, OPDA-Trp, and OPDA-Ile as primary products and OPDA-Phe to a lesser extent. 2. Group III GH3s are likely involved in non-Asp OPDA conjugation in planta. 3. GH3-mediated conjugation supports cis-OPDA buffering rather than signaling activation.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GH3 acyl acid amido synthetase, Group III
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: GH3 family acyl acid amido synthetase
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May affect OPDA and JA conjugate partitioning.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Conjugates cis-OPDA mainly with Ala, Trp, and Ile and secondarily with Phe.
